steps: Vote to dissolve per operating agreement or majority of members
Wind up business affairs and settle debts
Pay all outstanding franchise taxes and fees
File Certificate of Cancellation with Division of Corporations
File final state tax returns with Delaware Division of Revenue
llc.notes: In Delaware, LLCs are 'cancelled' rather than 'dissolved.' All past due franchise taxes, penalties, and interest must be paid before filing.

steps: Board of directors adopts resolution recommending dissolution
Shareholders approve dissolution by majority vote
Wind up business affairs, notify creditors, and settle debts
File all outstanding franchise tax reports and pay all franchise taxes due
File Certificate of Dissolution with Division of Corporations
File final state tax returns with Delaware Division of Revenue
corporation.notes: Short form dissolution ($10 fee) is available for corporations that never issued shares or began business. Standard dissolution filing fee is $204. All franchise taxes must be current.

tax.notes: All franchise tax reports must be filed and all franchise taxes, penalties, and interest must be paid before dissolution can be processed. File final Delaware corporate income tax return.

requirements.employeeNotification: Follow federal WARN Act if 100+ employees
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify known creditors in writing; publish notice for unknown creditors
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Delaware registered agent must be maintained until dissolution is complete
requirements.windingUpPeriod: 3 years after dissolution to wind up affairs and settle claims
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: Majority vote of members (LLC) or shareholders (Corp) required

includes: Every required form with direct download links
